findings.title: MAAT framework narrative dominance across 20+ portfolio surfaces despite operator-stated minor/experimental positioning (Pair B consumer side)
findings.cross_repo_pair: Pair B with new-direction-2026 F-002 (AAF-NDM-007). Consumer side is 4× more prominent than producer side (20+ refs here vs 5 narrative refs sibling). Staleness propagated through content pipeline and amplified by favicon design choice.

snapshot_evidence.lines: L8 each on 11 deployed pages + 1 experiments page
snapshot_evidence.evidence: 12 instances of `assets/icons/maat_feather_16.svg` favicon: index.html L8, cv.html L8, contact.html L8, projects/{maat,dicee,scopecam,budget-triage,flux,llm-gateway}.html L8 each, experience/index.html L10, research/index.html L8, experiments/timeline-braid.html L8.
Lead paragraph MAAT mention (L44); CS theory mention (L76); nav card href (L117).
h3 heading MAAT mention.
Formal-methods → policy-verification reference to MAAT.
Project-name listing.
Dedicated page: title L6 + h1 L48. Page existence itself is the strongest flagship signal contradicting operator-stated minor/experimental.
Lead-paragraph names MAAT first of 'two primary projects'.

Sibling pinned-facts contract enumerates 6 pinned facts (title, Ph.D., B.A., institution, live project=Dicee, key publication) — MAAT is NOT in this list. Internal contradiction with consumer-side prominence here.
Phase 8 §3.1 inventory + classification: `stale-authoring-artifact` × 20+ surface instances.

findings.recommendation_boundary: Policy home is the HTML pages themselves (no code extraction possible for a static portfolio). Operator-deferred remediation per stated intent. Candidate edits: (a) change favicon to a neutral identity mark, (b) rebalance lead-paragraph + nav-card prominence, (c) optionally retire projects/maat.html or reframe as 'early work' with date stamp. Phase 10 records the FF candidate (FF-005) as manual-review-only — there is no extracted-policy home to lint against.

findings.title: DEV_GUIDE.md contradicts AGENTS.md design system on 6 axes + 3 broken-reference categories

snapshot_evidence.evidence: Spacing scale: --space-xs (4px) through --space-2xl (64px) 8-point grid.
Spacing scale: --space-1 through --space-5 rem-based. CONTRADICTS DEV_GUIDE.md.
Body typography Georgia serif; accent #4C70A0; hover #D4AF37; max-width 1200px; CSS file assets/styles.css.
Body typography system sans-serif; accent hsl(212 85% 45%); accent-2 hsl(160 55% 35%); --measure 72ch; CSS file assets/jeffrey.css. CONTRADICTS DEV_GUIDE.md on all 5 axes.

References research/maat.html + research/scopecam.html (don't exist; actual pages at projects/), academic/ tree (doesn't exist), legacy maat/+scopecam/ dirs (don't exist), assets/styles.css (doesn't exist), and a navigation HTML snippet linking to /research/ and /academic/ — /academic/ is not in vite.config.js entry list. 3 broken-reference categories.
Two contracts with producer-consumer-drift flag.
Phase 8 §3.2 classification: `contradictory-guidance` (6 axes) + `stale-authoring-artifact` (3 broken-reference categories). 9 total sub-citations.

findings.recommendation_boundary: AGENTS.md is the operator-declared canonical contract per CLAUDE.md L3. DEV_GUIDE.md options: (a) delete, (b) replace with one-line redirect stub, (c) supersede with dated 'see AGENTS.md' note, (d) merge accurate fragments (e.g., L130-135 deployment section) into AGENTS.md. Phase 10 does not decide; FF-007 (docs-cross-reference-check) prevents recurrence.

findings.title: Biome 2 rules at `warn` not `error` severity — CI passes with style violations

snapshot_evidence.evidence: rules.complexity.noImportantStyles: "warn"; rules.style.noDescendingSpecificity: "warn". `npx biome check .` exits 0 with warnings.
quality job runs `npx biome check .` only; no `--error-on-warnings` flag.
Phase 9 §3: CI passes with warnings. Documented progression-to-error remediation candidate.

findings.recommendation_boundary: Two-step: (a) fix underlying CSS issues (operator), (b) escalate `warn` → `error` in biome.json. Single-line edit per rule. FF-009 (biome-rule-severity-progression) prevents new rules being added at warn without an ADR-style justification.

findings.title: README.md L13 stale `MIT © 2025` copyright + L10-11 project list omits 4 of 6 deployed projects

snapshot_evidence.evidence: `MIT © 2025 Jeffrey Johnson` — current date 2026-05-22.
Lists only MAAT + ScopeCam as 'two primary projects'. Deployed projects per vite.config.js L23-37: 6 (maat, dicee, scopecam, budget-triage, flux, llm-gateway).
Phase 8 §3.4 classification: `stale-authoring-artifact` × 2 sub-items. L10-11 reinforces AAF-VGH-001.

findings.recommendation_boundary: L13: trivial single-line edit to current year-range (e.g., 2025-2026). L10-11: consolidated with F-001 (MAAT rebalance) operator decision. FF-010 (copyright-year-check) is a tiny CI grep.

findings.title: Working-tree drift between profile initial-capture rev (2c6924d) and audit start — resolved within profile cycle via 3 commits per evidence_extensions[0]

snapshot_evidence.evidence: 3 commits landed (daea00d CNAME + 6eaa529 MAAT integration + ee6c7e5 profile). resolved_audit_attention_flags: [AAF-VGH-005]. Working tree clean at audit start at HEAD ee6c7e5.
Phase 0 confirmed resolution; no commit-level drift.

findings.smoke_test_note: Status is `struck` (not active) per audit spec §10.5 — finding retained in smoke-test log for audit-trail but absent from final priority ranking. The drift it described was fully resolved within the profile cycle by 3 commits; current evidence at HEAD ee6c7e5 confirms working tree is clean.
findings.recommendation_boundary: No action required. Resolution captured in profile meta.evidence_extensions[0] per operator-approved same-day triage convention (matches meta-inventory's pattern).

findings.title: PostToolUse hook silent-error swallow — write-capable automation principal with zero observability

snapshot_evidence.evidence: PostToolUse hook on Write|Edit calls runs `npx biome format --write ${tool_input.file_path} 2>/dev/null; exit 0`. Silenced stderr + forced exit 0. 10-second timeout. Auto-approved on Write|Edit matcher.

Phase 7 §3.4 — single biggest observability gap in repo. Phase 6 A-04 flags ambient-authority + write-without-approval-policy + approval-not-enforced. Compound: write-capable automation + no provenance + no exit-code signal + no log + no event + no metric.

Phase 6 A-04 (PostToolUse hook): NO audit log of invocations. Approval gate G-post-tool-hook auto-approved on Write|Edit matcher with no operator confirmation. The only mitigation is git-recoverability of any change made (the hook only re-formats).

findings.recommendation_boundary: Single-file edit to .claude/settings.json. Replace `2>/dev/null; exit 0` with tee to `.claude/logs/posttooluse.log`, propagate biome's actual exit code, emit a structured event line (timestamp + file_path + biome_exit_code + biome_version). FF-001 (claude-code-hook-observability) + FF-002 (claude-code-hook-scope-declaration) make this enforceable across the family.

findings.title: experiments-sandbox bounded-context decision recorded — stateless-policy authority basis with manual cross-context promotion path

snapshot_evidence.evidence: Phase 2 §2.4: experiments-sandbox CONFIRMED as bounded context with stateless-policy authority basis. 17 files, 5 evidence types: build exclusion (vite.config.js L22-37), lint exclusion (biome.json L4), lifecycle declaration (.cursor/rules/html.mdc L39-41 + project.mdc L61 + AGENTS.md L23,139), filesystem evidence (17 files), active-use evidence (operator IS using sandbox).
Hand-authored prototypes + svg build pipeline + 3 governance-style docs. Manual hand-promotion path to portfolio-pages via experiments/braid-timeline.svg → experience/index.html L54.

findings.recommendation_boundary: DECISION RECORD — no remediation required. Open operator decision: should the manual cross-context promotion path (experiments → portfolio-pages via hand-copy of braid-timeline.svg) be formalized as an ADR? Phase 2 confirmed boundary; Phase 10 records the decision; future ADR catalog (see F-010) could capture it.

findings.title: Cross-repo content-pipeline 6-row producer-consumer contract — consumer side has zero validation (Pair A consumer side)
findings.cross_repo_pair: Pair A with new-direction-2026 F-003 (AAF-NDM-012, producer side). Both sides have no automated check.

snapshot_evidence.evidence: Sibling repo declares 6 source → destination mappings: CV-COMPREHENSIVE-EXTRACT.md → cv.html, PORTFOLIO-QUICK.md → index.html, ACTIVE-PROJECTS-PORTFOLIO.md → projects/*.html, RESEARCH-PUBLICATIONS.md → research/, timeline-component-spec.md → experience/, TEACHING-INVENTORY.md → cv.html.

Phase 4 §2.19: 11 deployed HTML pages consume the producer's source documents. NO source-revision provenance comment in destinations. NO build-time check that destinations match sources. Flag: content-provenance-position-missing.
Grep for `source:` / `pipeline:` / `new-direction-2026 @` comments in HTML head: NONE present.
Phase 9 §4.8: no eval surface asserts content freshness, source-revision provenance, or destination-source alignment.

findings.recommendation_boundary: Build-time enhancement: insert `<!-- source: new-direction-2026 @ <sha> -->` comment into each generated HTML head. Producer-side complement at sibling's audit (FF-006 there). Pair A both repos cooperate. FF-006 in this repo (html-source-revision-provenance-comment) is consumer-side enforceable; full pipeline check requires producer-consumer handshake.

findings.title: context7 MCP referenced in CLAUDE.md L27-33 but no .mcp.json — operator-installed at Claude Code global level, not project-scoped
findings.cross_repo_pair: Family-wide pattern; cross-cites sibling new-direction-2026 F-004 (AAF-NDM-008, 5 MCPs uninventoried). Sibling has 5 operator-installed MCPs uninventoried; vgh has 1 (context7). Same root cause: operator-global MCP installation without per-project manifests.

snapshot_evidence.evidence: Declares context7 MCP server with 2 tools (resolve-library-id, query-docs). 'Always call resolve-library-id first to get the correct library ID, then pass it to query-docs.'
Cross-reference to context7 MCP.
ls -la verified ABSENT: .mcp.json.
Phase 4 §2.18 confirmed: operator-installed, not project-scoped, not pinned. Flags: mcp-surface-uninventoried + tool-without-schema. Cross-repo Pair with sibling AAF-NDM-008.

findings.recommendation_boundary: Three operator options: (a) add `.mcp.json` that pins context7 + protocol version + tool schemas, (b) remove in-repo MCP declaration if purely operator-machine config, (c) demote CLAUDE.md MCP table to 'operator-installed; see <URL>' pointer. Phase 10 does not decide. FF-011 (mcp-project-scoping) + FF-012 (mcp-tool-schema-recorded) cover family-wide enforcement.

findings.title: 5 GitHub Actions @v* not @SHA-pinned — supply-chain authority surfaces unbounded (deploy-pages@v4 highest priority)

snapshot_evidence.evidence: 5 actions @major-tag-pinned, none @SHA-pinned: actions/checkout@v4 (L25, L48), actions/setup-node@v4 (L28, L51), actions/configure-pages@v4 (L64), actions/upload-pages-artifact@v3 (L68), actions/deploy-pages@v4 (L83).

Phase 6 A-10: deploy job permissions pages:write + id-token:write inherited workflow-root from L10-13. deploy-pages@v4 is the SINGLE action combining elevated permissions + OIDC token acquisition + outbound publish authority + major-tag pin. Other 4 actions are moderate-risk.
Phase 4 §2.4: flag version-drift + runtime-provenance-missing.

findings.recommendation_boundary: Two-part remediation: (a) SHA-pin all 5 actions (single grep + replace per action); (b) reduce workflow-root permissions to contents:read only and declare pages:write + id-token:write at deploy job level only. FF-003 (pin-github-actions-to-sha) + FF-004 (per-job-actions-permissions). Highest priority is deploy-pages@v4 (PUBLISH action with OIDC). Cross-repo applicable to any sibling repo adding Actions later.

findings.title: PR-time CI declared but required-for-merge gate is GitHub branch-protection rules external to repo (declared-vs-enforced caveat)

snapshot_evidence.evidence: PR-time runs quality+build but NOT deploy (`if: github.event_name != 'pull_request'` per L74). Job declarations are complete in-repo.
GitHub branch-protection-rule visibility is external to repo. In-repo declaration cannot prove gate is required-for-merge.

findings.smoke_test_note: Classification per §10.5: discrepancy between profile claim (declared CI gate) and external enforcement state (branch protection rules visible only to repo admin) is itself the finding. The audit cannot observe GitHub-API-level state from a reference-only Phase 10 re-grep. Status: drift-only.
findings.recommendation_boundary: Operator decision needed in remediation PR: (a) verify branch-protection rules at github.com require quality + build before merge, (b) document the rule state in AGENTS.md + a future ADR, (c) FF-008 (manual-review-only) names the recurring check. Carried forward to SUMMARY caveat per Phase 0 routing.

findings.title: THEMES.md declares multi-theme switching capability with no automated implementation; styles-academic.css does not exist; sed-scripts silently no-op

snapshot_evidence.evidence: L1-5: 'This site supports multiple visual themes. Switch between them by changing the stylesheet reference in HTML files.' L25-41: names styles-academic.css as second theme. L48-51: sed-script for switching.
Glob assets/styles-academic.css: NOT FOUND. The actual file is at experiments/themes/academic.css (different path, different bounded context).
Phase 8 §3.3 classification: `stale-authoring-artifact` + `contradictory-guidance`. 4 sub-citations.

findings.recommendation_boundary: Three operator options: (a) retire multi-theme claim from THEMES.md (keep as single-theme philosophy doc), (b) move experiments/themes/academic.css → assets/styles-academic.css and add HTML-level switcher, (c) document as-is reality (manual hand-edit; experimental prototype, not deployed). Phase 8 does not decide. FF-007 (docs-cross-reference-check) catches the L25 broken filename reference.

Pair A — content-pipeline.synthesis_layer: Pair A reconciled in cross-repo synthesis at meta-inventory/audit/2026-05-21/cross-repo-synthesis-2026-05-21.md §0.4

Pair B — MAAT positioning.synthesis_layer: Pair B reconciled in cross-repo synthesis at meta-inventory/audit/2026-05-21/cross-repo-synthesis-2026-05-21.md §0.4. Consumer side 4× more prominent than producer side (20+ refs vs 5).

MCP family pattern.synthesis_layer: Same root cause: operator-global MCP install without per-project manifest. Family-wide FF-011 + FF-012 cover both.

priority_ranking_top_10_active_only.title: Cross-repo content-pipeline consumer side no validation (AAF-VGH-008; Pair A)
